content: State-of-the-art 2024-2025 active learning agent memory systems can be rearchitected to treat the entire population of independent downstream task agents as a distributed, world-facing episodic memory layer for a root curiosity-driven active learning agent. This setup lets the root agent avoid the common scaling pitfall of collapsing local memory noise by offloading uncertain, unlabeled data points to the distributed agent population to explore and label in parallel, rather than storing them directly in its own finite memory.
